content: Been doing 16/8 for a while but never considered extending to 20/4. Your point about cognitive peak at 14-18 hours is interesting. Going to give this a try starting next week.

replies.content: Good luck! Take it slow—extend by 30 minutes every few days. Your body will adapt faster than you think.

content: How do you handle social situations where everyone is eating during your fasting window? This is my biggest struggle with longer fasts.
Rode a similar route last summer. The Tara Canyon section is absolutely incredible—some of the best roads in Europe. Did you stop at the Đurđevića Tara Bridge?

replies.content: Absolutely! Spent an hour there just taking it in. The view from the bridge is unreal—definitely a highlight of the trip.

content: This is exactly the kind of content I needed. Planning a similar trip next month. What was your daily budget like for accommodation and food?
Been following your training for a while. The progress you've made on one-arm pushups is inspiring. Quick question: how do you progress from archer pushups to full one-arm?

replies.content: Great question! The key is gradually reducing the assistance from your supporting arm. Start with fingertips, then just one finger, then just a knuckle touch for balance. Also, negatives are huge—jump up and lower under control. Check out my One-Arm Pullup Roadmap guide for the full progression system—it applies to pushups too!

content: Do you ever incorporate weights into your training, or is it 100% bodyweight?
This article hit me at the right time. I've been struggling with consistency, always waiting to 'feel like it.' Going to implement the 2-minute rule starting today.
The identity-based habits concept is powerful. Instead of 'I'm trying to work out more,' I've started saying 'I'm an athlete.' The shift in mindset is real.
Currently at 12 strict pullups. This roadmap gives me hope that the OAP is achievable. Starting Phase 1 next week!
How important is grip strength for the one-arm pullup? Should I be doing specific grip work alongside this program?

replies.content: Grip is crucial but often develops naturally through the training. The weighted pullups and one-arm hangs will build plenty of grip strength. If you want extra work, add dead hangs after your sessions and farmer's walks on off days.
